commands.review_summary:
The PR trims the required `web_fetch` URL argument and removes whitespace immediately after `http://` or `https://` before the fetch path validates and executes it.

PR surface: Source +3. Total +3 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection and a Node URL parser probe: current main trims only the outside of the string, then `new URL('https:// docs.openclaw.ai')` throws before fetch execution. I did not run the full tool test suite in this read-only review.

target.title: fix(tools): sanitize url parameter in web_fetch to handle model formatting errors
target.body:
### Description

This PR fixes a bug where `web_fetch` fails when the LLM generates a URL containing whitespaces (e.g., `"https:// docs.openclaw.ai"`).

### Rationale

LLMs (especially smaller models) sometimes introduce spaces after protocol markers or surrounding the URL argument. Rather than failing immediately, we should clean up the input URL by:
1. Trimming leading/trailing whitespace.
2. Stripping spaces immediately after `http://` or `https://`.

This prevents hard errors from being returned to the user chat client.

Fixes #91651
